title: adenophorus hillebrandii
intro: Adenophorus hillebrandii, also known as Kama'aha or Haleakala fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Adenophorus hillebrandii include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. In traditional Hawaiian herbal medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although modern research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
